item.title: Japanese hardware stocks are having a moment — again
item.description: The current surge plays to the country’s industrial strengths

item.title: SpaceX pitches investors $1.8tn valuation in historic IPO
item.description: Rocket and AI group is seeking to raise up to $86bn in biggest Wall Street debut of all time

item.title: SpaceX wins tax exemption for $55bn AI chip plant despite local backlash
item.description: Elon Musk’s Terafab plant sparks fierce opposition and threat of legal action from residents of Texas county

item.title: Senior official from Germany’s AfD meets top Kremlin associates
item.description: Executives hold talks with far-right party, which leads German polls and supports restarting gas flows through Nord Stream

item.title: Saudi wealth fund replaces foreign CEOs with locals
item.description: Public Investment Fund focuses on domestic investments ahead of country hosting 2034 World Cup

item.title: UK Treasury set to take control of fighter jet spending after defence ministry mishaps
item.description: Move comes as Keir Starmer prepares to announce £15bn increase in military expenditure

item.title: Trump’s proposed grant rules could destroy US science, researcher warns
item.description: Additional funding controls by Office of Management and Budget will make new discoveries less likely, say critics

item.title: Trump’s Iran war drains US oil stocks to lowest level since 2004
item.description: Industry warns prices could soon jump as inventories reach ‘critically low’ threshold

item.title: Ebola spreads to new areas of DR Congo as outbreak widens
item.description: One infected person in Uganda is known to have travelled to the United Arab Emirates before falling ill

item.title: Europe’s tightrope walk on tech sovereignty
item.description: EU should be wary of curbing access to US providers as it tries to nurture its own

item.title: UK government urges companies to share data about AI effects on workforce
item.description: Move comes amid concerns that AI will exacerbate youth unemployment

item.title: Barclays hopes to boost UK economy with enlightened self-interest
item.description: Bank wants the government to make changes that encourage more foreign companies to manage their cash from the UK

item.title: Elon Musk shifts social media focus from SpaceX to UK far-right politics
item.description: Tech billionaire writes 110 posts, retweets and replies about British affairs in space of week

item.title: UK government adviser urges clarity on Palantir access to NHS patient data
item.description: Move by Nicola Byrne comes after FT reported that health service had agreed new ‘admin’ role for some external staff

item.title: Employers step in to fill the AI education gap
item.description: School and university curricula aren’t nimble enough to keep up with rapid tech transformation

item.title: Gulf states in talks for oil pipelines to bypass Hormuz
item.description: Oil exporters seek alternative ways to export, as crucial waterway has been disrupted for over three months

item.title: MP sues Musk’s xAI in UK test case over fake sexual images
item.description: Legal claim by Jess Asato challenges whether AI model-makers are liable for what their systems produce

item.title: Former UBS private credit fund says law firm helped defraud it of $145mn
item.description: Fund alleges Pillsbury Winthrop lawyer sent it ‘fake financials’ to secure loans linked to Aspiration Partners

item.title: Nissan signs deal with China’s Chery for Sunderland car production
item.description: Future of UK’s largest car plant was thrown into doubt by massive restructuring of Japanese group

item.title: Google parent Alphabet upsizes record-breaking equity raise to $85bn
item.description: Tech giant launches first stock offering in more than two decades in effort to fund AI spending

item.title: Vanguard fund becomes first ETF to top $1tn in assets
item.description: Rapid growth shows volume of passive money ready to buy IPOs such as SpaceX and Anthropic

item.title: Meta bets on AI agents to unlock WhatsApp revenues
item.description: Mark Zuckerberg expands group’s AI push as it seeks to turn messaging app into bigger business

item.title: Warsh needs to separate his roles at the Fed
item.description: This is how the new chair should approach his first rate-setting meeting

item.title: UK to spend £1.3bn supporting Bedford theme park project
item.description: Comcast’s £5bn Universal resort is due to open in 2031 and employ 20,000 during construction

item.title: Sorry Marc, it’s just not that big
item.description: Sizing up private credit

item.title: Can batteries halt India’s coal power growth?
item.description: Adani investment reflects shifting economics of clean baseload power

item.title: Unilever CEO defends food deal saying staff ‘not paid to be lazy’
item.description: Fernando Fernández argues $66bn combination of its food business with McCormick is right step despite upheaval

item.title: FirstFT: US proposes tariffs on 60 countries after forced labour probe
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: CBS fires Scott Pelley and Kevin Warsh prepares to roll back Fed’s forward guidance

item.title: Mobile gaming companies lash out at Brussels
item.description: Makers of ‘Clash of Clans’, ‘Candy Crush Saga’ and ‘Subway Surfers’ warn EU proposals could throttle rare tech success

item.title: Partners Group limits withdrawals in private equity fund for wealthy individuals
item.description: Swiss private capital firm caps redemptions from its $8.6bn flagship vehicle
